name: Certo® Apricot-Pineapple Jam
recipeIngredient: 3 cups prepared fruit (about 1 pound fully ripe apricots and 1/2 fully ripe medium pineapple)
recipeInstructions:
1. Fill a large pot with water and bring to a boil.
2. Place jars on a rack in the boiling water for 10 minutes.
3. Put flat lids in a saucepan filled with hot water; bring to a boil, then remove from heat.
4. Allow jars and lids to stand in the hot water until ready to fill.
5. Drain jars well before filling.
6. Remove pits (do not peel) and finely chop apricots.
7. Measure 2 cups into a 6- or 8-quart saucepot.
8. Pare, core, and finely chop pineapple; measure 1 cup and add to the apricots in the saucepot.
9. Stir in lemon juice and Fruit Protector (if using).
10. In a separate bowl, measure sugar with a spatula until level is reached for an exact measure.
11. Stir the measured sugar into the fruit mixture in the saucepot.
12. Add margarine; stir.
13. Bring the mixture to a full rolling boil on high heat, stirring constantly.
14. Quickly stir in the pectin; return to a full rolling boil and maintain it for exactly 1 minute, stirring constantly.
15. Remove from heat and skim off any foam with a metal spoon.
16. Ladle quickly into prepared jars, filling to within 1/8 inch of the top.
17. Wipe jar rims and threads; cover with two-piece lids.
18. Screw bands tightly onto the jars.
19. Invert jars for minutes, then turn them upright.
20. After jars are cool, check seals. Alternatively, follow the water bath method recommended by the USDA.
